info.description:
TraceRTM is a comprehensive requirements traceability management system that helps teams
track relationships between requirements, features, and tasks throughout the development lifecycle.

## Features
- Project and Item Management
- Link Management for traceability
- AI Agent Coordination
- Graph-based relationship analysis
- Full-text search with pgvector
- Real-time updates via WebSocket and NATS

## Authentication
Currently supports WorkOS AuthKit. Set Authorization header with Bearer token.

equivalence_ids.description: EquivalenceIDs are the IDs to confirm

reason.description: Reason is optional context for all confirmations

confirmed.description: Confirmed is the number of successfully confirmed links

errors.description: Errors maps failed IDs to error messages

failed.description: Failed is the number that failed to confirm

links.description: Links are the confirmed equivalence links

equivalence_ids.description: EquivalenceIDs are the IDs to reject

reason.description: Reason explains why equivalences were rejected

errors.description: Errors maps failed IDs to error messages

failed.description: Failed is the number that failed to reject

rejected.description: Rejected is the number of successfully rejected equivalences

file_path.description: FilePath is the source file path

line_end.description: LineEnd is the ending line number

line_start.description: LineStart is the starting line number

symbol_type.description: SymbolType is the symbol type (function, class, method, etc.)

reason.description: Reason is optional context for the confirmation

link.description: Link is the confirmed equivalence link

parent_concept_id.description: ParentConceptID links to a parent concept (optional)

tags.description: Tags are optional tags for organization

concept.description: Concept is the created canonical concept

created_at.description: CreatedAt is when the concept was created

confidence.description: Confidence is the confidence of this projection (0.0-1.0)

item_id.description: ItemID is the item being projected onto the canonical concept

perspective.description: Perspective is the view perspective (e.g., code, requirements, design)

role.description: Role is the role of this projection (primary, related, derived)

source.description: Source indicates how the projection was detected/confirmed

created_at.description: CreatedAt is when the projection was created

candidate_pool.description: CandidatePool specifies which items to check (if nil, checks all items in project)

max_results.description: MaxResults limits the number of suggestions returned

min_confidence.description: MinConfidence filters suggestions below this threshold (0.0-1.0)

project_id.description: ProjectID is the project to search for equivalences within

source_item_id.description: SourceItemID is the item to find equivalences for

strategies.description:
Strategies specifies which detection strategies to use
If empty, all strategies are used

suggestions.description: Suggestions are the detected equivalence suggestions

average_confidence.description: AverageConfidence is the mean confidence across all findings

duration_ms.description: DurationMs is detection time in milliseconds

equivalences_found.description: EquivalencesFound is the number of suggestions created

total_items_scanned.description: TotalItemsScanned is the number of items checked

concept.description: Concept is the canonical concept details

id.description: ID is the item unique identifier

data.description: Data is the list of equivalence links

has_more.description: HasMore indicates if there are more results

limit.description: Limit is the limit used in the request

offset.description: Offset is the offset used in the request

total.description: Total is the total number of records matching the filter

reason.description: Reason explains why the equivalence was rejected

handlers.HealthResponse.description: Health check response with service status

ApiKeyAuth.description: Enter your bearer token in the format: Bearer {token}
ApiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
